description: Named collections of default role assignments that can be applied to workspaces or channels to customize the permission baseline for all members, replacing system-wide role defaults with...

actors.name: System Administrator
actors.description: Creates, updates, deletes, and assigns permission schemes

fields.name: scheme_id
fields.label: Unique identifier for the permission scheme

fields.name: scope
fields.label: What type of scope this scheme applies to

fields.name: default_team_admin_role
fields.label: Role ID applied to team administrators in workspaces using this scheme

fields.name: default_team_user_role
fields.label: Role ID applied to regular members in workspaces using this scheme

fields.name: default_team_guest_role
fields.label: Role ID applied to guests in workspaces using this scheme

fields.name: default_channel_admin_role
fields.label: Role ID applied to channel administrators in channels using this scheme

fields.name: default_channel_user_role
fields.label: Role ID applied to regular members in channels using this scheme

fields.name: default_channel_guest_role
fields.label: Role ID applied to guests in channels using this scheme

values.name: active
values.description: Scheme available for assignment to workspaces or channels

values.name: deleted
values.description: Scheme soft-deleted; assignments are cleared; affected scopes revert to system defaults

transitions.description: Administrator deletes the scheme; all team/channel assignments removed atomically

rules.rule_01: A scheme may have either team scope (defines team-level defaults) or channel scope (defines channel-level defaults) but not both.
rules.rule_02: Each workspace may have at most one scheme assigned; each channel may have at most one scheme assigned.
rules.rule_03: When a scheme is assigned to a workspace, new members joining that workspace receive the scheme's default role for their membership type (admin, user, or guest).
rules.rule_04: Scheme-managed roles may not be directly assigned or removed as explicit roles; they are controlled exclusively through scheme assignment.
rules.rule_05: System-level permissions cannot be overridden by team or channel schemes.
rules.rule_06: Permission resolution is additive across scopes — if any role grants a permission at any scope, access is granted.
rules.rule_07: Deleting a scheme removes all team and channel assignments in the same transaction; affected scopes immediately revert to system defaults.
rules.rule_08: All referenced role IDs in a scheme must exist at the time the scheme is created or updated.
rules.rule_09: When scheme permissions are updated, changes take effect immediately for all current role holders.
rules.rule_10: The system provides a reset mechanism that wipes all custom schemes and roles, restoring factory defaults; this is typically used during major permission system migrations.

then.description: Scheme record created with all specified default role references

scheme_created.result: Scheme available for assignment to workspaces or channels

then.description: Workspace now references this scheme for default roles

scheme_assigned_to_workspace.result: All future members joining this workspace receive the scheme's default roles; existing members are updated to use scheme roles

then.description: Channel now references this scheme for member role defaults

scheme_assigned_to_channel.result: Channel members receive the scheme's channel-level role defaults

given: new permissions list contains only valid permission IDs for the scheme's scope

then.description: Role permission sets updated; changes propagate to all role holders

scheme_permissions_updated.result: Permission changes take effect immediately across all workspaces and channels using this scheme

scheme_deleted.result: All assignments removed; affected scopes revert to system permission defaults

given: new scheme name matches an existing active scheme

errors.message: A permission scheme with that name already exists.
Scheme scope must be either 'team' or 'channel'.
One or more referenced role IDs do not exist.
Scheme description must be 1024 characters or fewer.

events.name: scheme.created
events.description: A new permission scheme was created

events.name: scheme.updated
events.description: Scheme settings or role permissions were modified

events.name: scheme.deleted
events.description: Permission scheme was deleted and all assignments cleared

events.name: scheme.assigned_to_workspace
events.description: Scheme applied to a workspace to customize member role defaults

events.name: scheme.assigned_to_channel
events.description: Scheme applied to a channel to customize member role defaults

related.reason: Schemes reference and configure roles; RBAC is the underlying mechanism
Workspaces reference schemes; scheme changes affect all members of assigned workspaces
Channel moderation patches permissions at the channel level, working alongside channel schemes

goals.description: Named collections of default role assignments that can be applied to workspaces or channels to customize the permission baseline for all members, replacing system-wide role defaults with...

human_checkpoints: before transitioning to a terminal state

tradeoffs.reason: access control must enforce least-privilege principle

invariants: error messages never expose internal system details
state transitions follow the defined state machine — no illegal transitions
